extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), which fires iff polypharmacy AND low_ejection_fraction AND ckd_dialysis AND hypertension are all true. rec_drug_b is set true by r4 (priority 50), which fires iff polypharmacy is true. r4's condition (polypharmacy) is implied by r3's condition, so whenever r3 fires, r4 also fires. Because r3 and r4 target different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), there is no priority/conflict interaction between them — each output is resolved independently, and both get set to true. Rules r1, r2, r5 only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. With polypharmacy=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, ckd_dialysis=true, hypertension=true (others false), r3 sets rec_drug_a=true and r4 sets rec_drug_b=true, making (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) true. This violates mutual exclusion.
